unit: transmission event from a superspreading node
variation: individual contact patterns produce heavy-tailed distributions of secondary infections
selection: pathogens that exploit high-contact hosts propagate faster than those relying on average transmission
fitness: effective reproduction number driven by variance in individual infectiousness
boundary: loses predictive power when contact networks are homogeneous or when superspreading events are suppressed by isolation

name: Antigenic Drift Memory Erosion Theory
variation: point mutations in surface proteins create a cloud of related but immunologically distinct variants
selection: variants that escape population immunity spread; those recognized by prevalent antibodies are cleared
fitness: immune evasion capacity measured as reduction in cross-reactive neutralization
boundary: fails when antigenic space is exhausted or when immune memory is broad enough to cover the entire drift trajectory

name: Zoonotic Spillover Gradient Theory
variation: ecological disturbance creates variable contact rates between wildlife reservoirs and human populations
selection: pathogens with broader host tropism and higher environmental stability cross the species barrier more frequently
fitness: probability of sustained human-to-human transmission after initial spillover
boundary: breaks down when reservoir and human populations are fully separated or when spillover pathogens cannot adapt to human receptors

name: Behavioral Immunity Feedback Theory
unit: individual behavioral change in response to perceived disease risk
variation: heterogeneous risk perception leads to divergent protective behaviors across a population
selection: behaviors that reduce infection risk persist; costly behaviors are abandoned as perceived risk drops
fitness: infection probability weighted against social and economic cost of behavioral modification
boundary: irrelevant when disease is asymptomatic and undetectable or when behavior has no effect on transmission

name: Epidemic Wavefront Resonance Theory
variation: population density gradients and mobility corridors create heterogeneous wavefront velocities
selection: wavefronts that encounter susceptible population clusters amplify; those hitting immune barriers attenuate
fitness: wavefront propagation speed and amplitude across geographic space
boundary: collapses in well-mixed populations where spatial structure is negligible

name: Pathogen Virulence Tradeoff Theory
variation: mutations alter virulence and transmissibility in coupled but opposing directions
selection: highly virulent strains kill hosts before transmitting; low-virulence strains are outcompeted within hosts
fitness: lifetime transmission potential balancing within-host replication against between-host spread
boundary: fails when transmission occurs post-mortem or when virulence and transmission are uncoupled

name: Nosocomial Amplification Hierarchy Theory
variation: hospitals vary in infection control capacity, patient density, and staff mobility patterns
selection: facilities with weak controls become amplification hubs that seed community outbreaks
fitness: facility-level reproduction number determining whether nosocomial chains sustain or extinguish
boundary: irrelevant for pathogens that do not survive in clinical environments or that spread primarily outdoors

name: Syndemic Coupling Theory
unit: co-occurring disease pair within a population
variation: social and biological conditions create variable susceptibility to multiple diseases simultaneously
selection: populations experiencing disease clustering face compounding morbidity that exceeds additive predictions
fitness: excess disease burden attributable to synergistic interaction between co-circulating conditions
boundary: breaks down when diseases are biologically independent and share no common social determinants

name: Vaccination Threshold Oscillation Theory
variation: vaccine uptake fluctuates as perceived risk changes with disease incidence
selection: high coverage reduces disease, reducing perceived risk, reducing uptake, allowing resurgence
fitness: stable herd immunity maintenance versus boom-bust cycling of coverage and outbreaks
boundary: irrelevant when vaccination is mandatory with full compliance or when disease has no visible symptoms

name: Immune Landscape Drift Theory
variation: birth cohorts, prior exposures, and vaccination histories create a heterogeneous immune landscape
selection: pathogen lineages that find gaps in the population immune landscape expand into those niches
fitness: effective susceptible fraction available to a given pathogen lineage
boundary: loses relevance when cross-immunity is complete or when immune memory does not wane

name: Load Path Redundancy Theory
variation: alternative load paths emerge from material heterogeneity and connection geometry
selection: load paths that efficiently transfer forces to foundations persist; overstressed paths fail and redistribute load
fitness: structural resilience measured as capacity to sustain function after local member failure
boundary: fails in statically determinate structures where no alternative load paths exist

name: Thermal Envelope Phase Theory
variation: material conductivity, thickness, and vapor permeability vary across envelope layers
selection: assemblies where moisture condenses within the envelope degrade; dry assemblies persist
fitness: minimized energy loss while preventing condensation at any layer interface
boundary: irrelevant in climates with no temperature differential across the envelope or in open-air structures

name: Circulation Hierarchy Emergence Theory
variation: corridor widths, intersections, and destinations create heterogeneous movement affordances
selection: routes that minimize travel time and cognitive load attract traffic; underused routes are closed or repurposed
fitness: aggregate wayfinding efficiency across all building occupants
boundary: collapses in single-room structures or when all destinations are equidistant from all origins

name: Daylight Gradient Partitioning Theory
unit: daylight zone defined by illuminance contour
variation: window placement, depth, and surface reflectance create variable light gradients across floor plates
selection: program elements migrate to daylight zones matching their illuminance requirements over building lifetime
fitness: percentage of occupied hours where natural light meets task illuminance without glare
boundary: irrelevant in underground structures or at extreme latitudes during polar night

name: Adaptive Reuse Encoding Theory
unit: spatial module capable of functional reprogramming
variation: structural grid dimensions, floor-to-ceiling heights, and service access points vary across building stock
selection: buildings with dimensional properties matching new program requirements are reused; mismatched ones are demolished
fitness: number of distinct viable programs a spatial module can support across its lifespan
boundary: fails when demolition and new construction cost less than adaptation or when regulatory constraints prevent reuse

name: Acoustic Resonance Compartmentalization Theory
unit: acoustic zone defined by reverberation time
variation: room geometry, material absorption coefficients, and aperture sizes produce variable acoustic fields
selection: spaces where acoustic conditions conflict with program use are modified or abandoned
fitness: speech intelligibility and acoustic comfort matched to programmatic requirements
boundary: irrelevant in outdoor spaces or when electronic sound masking overrides architectural acoustics

name: Material Decay Succession Theory
unit: material assembly with defined service life
variation: materials degrade at different rates depending on exposure, loading, and maintenance regimes
selection: assemblies where short-lived components are inaccessible for replacement trigger cascading failures
fitness: total lifecycle cost minimized through alignment of component service lives with replacement accessibility
boundary: breaks down for monolithic structures with no component differentiation or for buildings with unlimited maintenance budgets

name: Settlement Pattern Network Theory
variation: plot sizes, setbacks, and access points create diverse relationships between buildings and public networks
selection: configurations that maximize frontage access and minimize infrastructure run lengths are replicated in development patterns
fitness: ratio of usable floor area to required infrastructure length serving the settlement
boundary: collapses in isolated buildings with no shared infrastructure or in megastructures that internalize all networks

name: Structural Symmetry Breaking Theory
unit: structural bay subject to asymmetric loading
variation: live loads, wind loads, and seismic forces introduce asymmetric demands on nominally symmetric structures
selection: symmetric designs that cannot accommodate asymmetric demands develop localized stress concentrations and crack
fitness: graceful performance degradation under asymmetric loading without catastrophic failure
boundary: irrelevant when all loads are perfectly symmetric or when the structure is designed as fully asymmetric from the outset

name: Warp-Weft Tension Balancing
variation: fluctuations in yarn tension, loom speed, and warp beam diameter
selection: intersections maintaining balanced biaxial tension survive as stable fabric; slack or over-tensioned regions produce defects and are rejected
fitness: fabric uniformity and tensile strength per unit area
boundary: yarn elasticity limits, loom mechanical tolerance, beam geometry

name: Felting Fiber Entanglement
variation: random fiber orientations and cuticle-scale interlocking under agitation, heat, and moisture
selection: junctions that form irreversible mechanical bonds persist; weakly coupled fibers work free
fitness: density of irreversible entanglement points per volume
boundary: fiber scale structure, pH, temperature, mechanical energy input

name: Dye Mordant Chemistry
variation: competing coordination bonds between metal ion mordants, dye chromophores, and fiber reactive sites
selection: complexes with strongest chelation and fiber affinity resist wash-out; weak bonds dissociate
fitness: color fastness rating under wash, light, and abrasion
boundary: pH range, mordant concentration, fiber surface chemistry, temperature

name: Spinning Draft Ratio Control
variation: differential roller speeds attenuate the roving, redistributing fiber alignment and count
selection: draft ratios producing target linear density and twist are retained; out-of-range settings cause breakage or slubs
fitness: yarn count consistency (CV%) and break strength
boundary: staple length, fiber friction, roller spacing, twist multiplier

name: Jacquard Binary Patterning
variation: combinatorial binary states across thousands of hooks per pick
selection: only lift sequences encoding the target pattern are programmed; all other permutations are excluded by the punch card / digital control
fitness: pattern fidelity to design at full repeat resolution
boundary: hook count, harness capacity, weft color palette, card storage

name: Knit Stitch Topology
variation: knit, purl, tuck, miss, and transfer stitches create distinct loop topologies
selection: stitch combinations producing desired stretch, drape, and structural integrity propagate through the fabric; unstable configurations unravel
fitness: fabric extensibility, recovery, and resistance to laddering
boundary: yarn bending rigidity, needle gauge, loop length, stitch density

name: Selvage Edge Stability
variation: different edge constructions (leno, tucked, fused) compete to prevent fraying under tension
selection: edge structures that lock outermost weft loops survive cutting and handling; weak edges unravel
fitness: edge integrity under lateral stress and repeated flexing
boundary: fabric width, weft insertion method, yarn type, downstream finishing

name: Mercerization Swelling
variation: sodium hydroxide concentration gradients cause differential swelling and crystalline rearrangement across fiber cross-section
selection: crystallites converting from cellulose I to cellulose II gain luster, strength, and dye affinity; unconverted regions remain dull
fitness: percentage conversion to cellulose II and resulting tensile strength gain
boundary: NaOH concentration, temperature, tension during treatment, fiber maturity

name: Nonwoven Spunbond Bonding
variation: random fiber laydown orientation and calender nip temperature/pressure variations
selection: bond points achieving sufficient polymer fusion without fiber degradation persist; under-bonded regions delaminate, over-bonded regions embrittle
fitness: tensile strength-to-weight ratio and uniformity index
boundary: polymer melt point, line speed, calender pattern, basis weight

name: Harmonic Series Overtone Structure
variation: integer multiples of fundamental frequency produced by vibrating bodies with different geometries and materials
selection: partials reinforced by resonant body geometry dominate the spectrum; damped partials decay rapidly
fitness: relative amplitude and persistence of each partial in the steady-state spectrum
boundary: string/air column length, material density, damping coefficient, excitation method

name: Counterpoint Voice Leading Rules
variation: all possible melodic motions (parallel, contrary, oblique, similar) between consecutive intervals
selection: transitions preserving independence, consonance at strong beats, and avoiding parallel fifths/octaves are retained; forbidden motions are eliminated by rule
fitness: contrapuntal smoothness and voice independence as judged by species rules
boundary: number of voices, mode/key, range limits, rhythmic hierarchy

name: Rhythmic Entrainment
variation: slight timing deviations (micro-timing) around an isochronous grid across performers
selection: onsets converging toward shared phase lock persist as the groove; outlier timings are corrected by auditory feedback
fitness: tightness of phase alignment across ensemble members
boundary: tempo range, human motor-timing jitter, latency of auditory feedback loop

name: Sonata Form Development-Recapitulation
variation: fragmentation, modulation, inversion, and reharmonization of exposition themes during development
selection: transformations that heighten tonal tension are elaborated; the recapitulation resolves all themes to the tonic, selecting for tonal unity
fitness: narrative coherence and satisfying tonal resolution
boundary: key scheme conventions, audience expectation, movement duration

name: Modal Interchange / Borrowed Chords
variation: chords borrowed from parallel modes (major / minor, Lydian, Phrygian, etc.) introduced into a diatonic progression
selection: borrowed chords that create expressive color without derailing tonal center are retained; those causing ambiguity are resolved or avoided
fitness: harmonic expressiveness while maintaining key intelligibility
boundary: number of available parallel modes, voice-leading smoothness, listener tonal expectation

name: Twelve-Tone Serialism
variation: 48 row forms (prime, retrograde, inversion, retrograde-inversion x 12 transpositions) provide combinatorial possibilities
selection: row forms chosen to maximize intervallic interest, aggregate completion, and hexachordal combinatoriality
fitness: chromatic saturation and avoidance of tonal implication per the composer's aesthetic
boundary: strict ordering rule (all 12 before repeat), row partitioning constraints, total chromatic aggregate

name: Microtonality / Just Intonation
variation: rational frequency ratios (small-integer vs. irrational tempered approximations) across a tuning lattice
selection: ratios minimizing beating and maximizing consonance for a given harmonic context are favored; wolf intervals are avoided or redistributed
fitness: psychoacoustic consonance (simplicity of frequency ratio)
boundary: keyboard/instrument fixed pitch constraints, modulation range, comma drift accumulation

name: Call-and-Response Antiphony
variation: response varies in melodic contour, rhythmic augmentation, and harmonic elaboration relative to the call
selection: responses that complete the call's harmonic and rhythmic implication are reinforced by communal repetition; non-completing responses are dropped
fitness: communal participation rate and phrase memorability
boundary: phrase length, tonal vocabulary of the tradition, performer count, breath capacity

name: Timbre Spectral Envelope Shaping
unit: spectral bin (frequency-amplitude pair in FFT frame)
variation: formant positions, attack transients, and noise components differ across instruments and synthesis parameters
selection: spectral envelopes matching perceptual timbre targets are shaped by filter design, material choice, or synthesis algorithm; undesired partials are attenuated
fitness: perceptual match to target timbre identity
boundary: physical resonator geometry, filter order, sampling rate, psychoacoustic masking

name: Coriolis-Driven Cyclone Formation
variation: random convective disturbances and pressure perturbations across warm ocean surfaces
selection: vortices gaining sufficient rotation from Coriolis deflection and sustained by latent heat flux intensify; sub-threshold disturbances dissipate
fitness: sustained central pressure drop and organized eyewall convection
boundary: sea surface temperature >= 26.5C, low wind shear, sufficient Coriolis parameter (latitude > ~5 degrees)

name: Convective Available Potential Energy (CAPE)
unit: air parcel lifted from boundary layer
variation: parcels with varying temperature, moisture, and initial altitude compete for buoyancy advantage
selection: parcels exceeding the level of free convection accelerate upward; parcels with insufficient buoyancy remain capped by the inversion
fitness: maximum updraft velocity (proportional to sqrt(2*CAPE))
boundary: convective inhibition (CIN), lifted condensation level, equilibrium level height

name: Orographic Precipitation
unit: moist air column forced over terrain
variation: varying moisture content, wind speed, and approach angle of air masses encountering mountain barriers
selection: air columns forced above the lifting condensation level precipitate on windward slopes; leeward descent produces dry rain-shadow
fitness: precipitation rate on windward slope per unit moisture flux
boundary: mountain height and slope, prevailing wind direction, upstream moisture availability, freezing level

name: El Nino-Southern Oscillation
unit: coupled ocean-atmosphere state (SST anomaly + Walker circulation phase)
variation: stochastic westerly wind bursts and thermocline depth fluctuations in the equatorial Pacific
selection: positive Bjerknes feedback amplifies warm (El Nino) or cold (La Nina) anomalies once a threshold SST gradient shift occurs; delayed negative feedback from oceanic Rossby waves resets the cycle
fitness: amplitude and duration of SST anomaly in Nino 3.4 region
boundary: thermocline depth, mean trade wind strength, Pacific basin geometry, recharge-discharge timescale

name: Atmospheric River Moisture Transport
variation: filaments of varying width, moisture content, and velocity form along different extratropical cyclone warm sectors
selection: filaments exceeding IVT thresholds (~250 kg/m/s) and making landfall produce extreme precipitation; sub-threshold filaments dissipate or miss coastlines
fitness: total precipitable water delivered to landfall target per event
boundary: tropical moisture source strength, jet stream steering, orographic amplification at landfall

name: Temperature Inversion Trapping
unit: boundary layer air mass beneath inversion lid
variation: varying nocturnal radiative cooling rates, terrain configurations, and surface emissions produce different inversion strengths and heights
selection: inversions strong enough to suppress vertical mixing trap pollutants and moisture below the lid; weak inversions are broken by solar heating or mechanical turbulence
fitness: inversion persistence (hours) and concentration ratio of trapped constituents vs. free atmosphere
boundary: surface radiation budget, wind speed, terrain basin geometry, cloud cover

name: Jet Stream Rossby Wave Propagation
variation: wave amplitude, wavelength, and phase speed vary with meridional temperature gradient and jet speed
selection: wavelengths satisfying the Rossby dispersion relation propagate westward relative to the flow; resonant wave numbers amplify and steer surface weather; non-resonant waves are damped
fitness: wave amplitude persistence and downstream surface weather impact
boundary: beta parameter (planetary vorticity gradient), jet core speed, land-sea thermal contrast, topographic forcing

name: Mesoscale Convective Complex Self-Organization
unit: individual thunderstorm cell within the complex
variation: cells initiate at random locations along outflow boundaries with varying updraft strengths and moisture access
selection: cells generating cold-pool outflows that trigger new convection on their leading edge propagate the system; isolated cells without regenerative feedback dissipate
fitness: system longevity (>6 hours) and continuous cold-cloud shield area (>100,000 km2)
boundary: low-level jet moisture supply, nocturnal stabilization, wind shear profile, large-scale forcing

name: Fog Formation (Radiation vs. Advection)
variation: radiative cooling of ground (radiation fog) vs. warm moist air advecting over cold surface (advection fog) compete as saturation mechanisms
selection: conditions sustaining dew-point depression <= 0 at surface produce persistent fog; conditions where turbulent mixing or solar heating raises temperature above dew point dissipate fog
fitness: fog duration and horizontal visibility reduction
boundary: surface type, wind speed (calm for radiation, moderate for advection), moisture availability, cloud cover

name: Precedent Fitness Landscape Theory
variation: reinterpretation of prior rulings across jurisdictions and fact patterns
selection: appellate review and citation frequency by subsequent courts
fitness: maximized doctrinal coherence and applicability across novel cases
boundary: breaks down when legislative override or constitutional amendment renders precedent moot

name: Statutory Decay Half-Life Theory
variation: changing social norms, technological shifts, and economic conditions erode statutory relevance
selection: judicial willingness to apply versus distinguish or narrowly construe aging statutes
fitness: minimized gap between statutory text and contemporary societal expectations
boundary: breaks down for constitutional provisions designed to be timeless or deliberately vague

name: Legal Norm Oscillation Theory
unit: doctrinal norm (e.g., strict liability vs. negligence standard)
variation: pendulum swings between individual rights emphasis and collective welfare emphasis across court eras
selection: political composition of courts and shifting public sentiment
fitness: maximized perceived legitimacy of the legal system by the governed population
boundary: breaks down in authoritarian systems where judicial independence is absent

name: Jurisdictional Modularity Theory
unit: jurisdictional legal module (federal, state, municipal rule-set)
variation: independent legislative experimentation across federated jurisdictions
selection: migration of citizens and capital toward more favorable legal environments
fitness: maximized regulatory efficiency and citizen satisfaction within a jurisdiction
boundary: breaks down when preemption doctrine or international treaty obligations override local variation

name: Contractual Information Compression Theory
variation: diverse drafting conventions, boilerplate evolution, and bespoke negotiation across industries
selection: litigation outcomes that punish ambiguous or unenforceable clauses
fitness: maximized enforceability per unit of contractual text complexity
boundary: breaks down when courts impose implied terms or unconscionability doctrines that override explicit text

name: Rights Emergence Threshold Theory
variation: novel rights claims arising from technological change, social movements, and philosophical discourse
selection: judicial recognition, legislative codification, and international norm adoption
fitness: maximized breadth of recognized legal personhood and protected interests
boundary: breaks down when rights claims conflict irreconcilably and no meta-principle resolves priority

name: Legal System Network Topology Theory
variation: differential citation patterns across courts, treatises, and legal traditions
selection: hub authorities accumulate disproportionate citation weight via preferential attachment
fitness: maximized centrality and influence of a legal authority within the citation network
boundary: breaks down when paradigm shifts sever high-centrality nodes

name: Regulatory Scarcity Competition Theory
variation: differential applicant strategies, lobbying intensity, and compliance capacity
selection: administrative gatekeeping via review standards and resource allocation
fitness: maximized access to scarce regulated opportunities relative to compliance cost
boundary: breaks down when regulatory capture eliminates genuine competition for permissions

name: Dispute Resolution Phase Transition Theory
variation: escalation pathways from negotiation to mediation to arbitration to litigation
selection: cost-benefit calculus at each phase boundary determines whether disputes escalate or settle
fitness: minimized total resolution cost weighted by outcome satisfaction
boundary: breaks down when power asymmetries or emotional stakes make rational cost-benefit analysis inapplicable

name: Coral Holobiont Feedback Theory
unit: coral holobiont (coral host plus symbiotic microbiome)
variation: stochastic acquisition and shuffling of Symbiodiniaceae clades and bacterial consortia
selection: thermal stress events differentially eliminate holobionts with maladapted symbiont communities
fitness: maximized photosynthetic yield and calcification rate under variable thermal regimes
boundary: breaks down when bleaching events exceed the adaptive capacity of any available symbiont combination

name: Deep-Sea Vent Chemosynthetic Gradient Theory
variation: spatial and temporal fluctuations in hydrogen sulfide, methane, and mineral concentrations
selection: metabolic efficiency of chemolithoautotrophs along steep geochemical gradients
fitness: maximized energy harvest per unit of toxic substrate exposure
boundary: breaks down when vent systems go extinct and chemosynthetic communities cannot disperse to new vents

name: Marine Larval Dispersal Lottery Theory
variation: stochastic ocean current transport, larval duration polymorphism, and settlement cue sensitivity
selection: post-settlement mortality from predation, competition, and habitat quality mismatch
fitness: maximized probability of settlement on suitable substrate within competency window
boundary: breaks down when habitat fragmentation exceeds maximum dispersal distance for all larval morphotypes

name: Mesopelagic Diel Vertical Migration Oscillation Theory
variation: species-specific migration amplitude, timing offsets, and predator avoidance strategies
selection: trade-off between foraging gain in euphotic zone and predation risk from visual hunters
fitness: maximized net energy intake minus predation mortality over a 24-hour cycle
boundary: breaks down in polar regions with continuous daylight or darkness eliminating the diel light cue

name: Kelp Forest Trophic Cascade Topology Theory
unit: trophic interaction link (predator-prey or grazer-producer pair)
variation: species composition shifts, behavioral modifications, and density-dependent interaction strengths
selection: keystone predator presence or absence restructures entire network topology
fitness: maximized community biomass stability and primary productivity
boundary: breaks down when multiple simultaneous stressors decouple trophic links

name: Ocean Acidification Calcification Threshold Theory
variation: interspecific and population-level variation in aragonite saturation tolerance and energetic buffering capacity
selection: dissolution pressure on organisms unable to maintain shell above energetic cost thresholds
fitness: minimized metabolic cost of calcification per unit of structural integrity maintained
boundary: breaks down when aragonite undersaturation becomes permanent and no compensatory mechanism is sufficient

name: Seagrass Meadow Clonal Memory Theory
variation: somatic mutations, epigenetic modifications, and rhizome branching architecture differences across clones
selection: differential survival of clonal fragments under disturbance regimes
fitness: maximized spatial persistence and recolonization speed after disturbance
boundary: breaks down when sexual reproduction is required for adaptation beyond epigenetic range

name: Marine Microbiome Horizontal Gene Flow Theory
unit: mobile genetic element (plasmid, transposon, phage-carried gene cassette)
variation: horizontal gene transfer events mediated by phage transduction, conjugation, and natural transformation
selection: environmental filtering for genes conferring resistance, tolerance, or novel metabolic pathways
fitness: maximized gene persistence across host lineages independent of vertical inheritance
boundary: breaks down when physical barriers limit microbial contact rates below transfer threshold

name: Cetacean Acoustic Niche Partitioning Theory
variation: frequency band occupation, call timing, and modulation pattern divergence among sympatric cetacean species
selection: signal interference from conspecifics, heterospecifics, and anthropogenic noise
fitness: maximized signal detection probability by intended receivers within occupied frequency band
boundary: breaks down when broadband anthropogenic noise saturates all available acoustic niches

name: Semantic Key Derivation Lattice Theory
variation: input entropy sources, salt diversity, and iteration count parameterization
selection: brute-force attack cost eliminates weak derivation paths
fitness: maximized computational cost for attacker per unit of defender computation
boundary: breaks down when quantum algorithms reduce the effective search space below practical security margins

name: Cipher Suite Evolutionary Pressure Theory
unit: cipher suite (algorithm + key size + mode combination)
variation: new algorithm proposals, implementation variants, and parameter choices across standards bodies
selection: cryptanalytic breakthroughs, side-channel attacks, and performance benchmarks deprecate weak suites
fitness: maximized security margin per unit of computational and bandwidth overhead
boundary: breaks down during cryptographic transitions when legacy compatibility forces retention of broken suites

name: Zero-Knowledge Proof Compression Theory
variation: different proof systems (Schnorr, Groth16, PLONK, STARK) offering distinct size-time-trust tradeoffs
selection: verifier rejection of proofs exceeding acceptable size or verification time thresholds
fitness: minimized proof size and verification time while preserving soundness and zero-knowledge properties
boundary: breaks down when the statement being proved is too complex for any known proof system to handle efficiently

name: Side-Channel Leakage Topology Theory
unit: observable side-channel signal (timing, power, electromagnetic emission)
variation: implementation differences, hardware platform diversity, and environmental noise profiles
selection: attacker capability to distinguish signal from noise across measurement channels
fitness: maximized mutual information between secret state and observable leakage from attacker perspective
boundary: breaks down when constant-time implementations and hardware countermeasures reduce leakage below noise floor

name: Blockchain Consensus Equilibrium Theory
variation: heterogeneous computational resources, staking amounts, network latency, and strategic behavior
selection: protocol reward and punishment mechanisms (block rewards, slashing, difficulty adjustment)
fitness: maximized expected reward minus operational cost for honest participation strategy
boundary: breaks down when >50% of consensus power colludes or when reward economics incentivize deviation

name: Entropy Pool Accumulation Dynamics Theory
unit: entropy bit harvested from physical or system source
variation: diverse entropy sources (hardware RNG, interrupt timing, mouse movements) with different quality and rate
selection: entropy estimation algorithms reject low-quality or correlated samples
fitness: maximized available entropy rate while minimizing correlation between successive outputs
boundary: breaks down in virtualized or embedded environments with few physical entropy sources

name: Homomorphic Encryption Noise Budget Theory
variation: noise growth patterns differ across operation types (addition vs. multiplication) and scheme parameters
selection: decryption failure eliminates computation paths that exhaust the noise budget
fitness: maximized computational depth before noise budget exhaustion
boundary: breaks down when required circuit depth exceeds bootstrapping capability

name: Post-Quantum Migration Phase Transition Theory
variation: different migration strategies (hybrid, full replacement, crypto-agility) across organizations
selection: harvest-now-decrypt-later threat model pressures early adopters; compliance mandates force laggards
fitness: minimized window of quantum vulnerability weighted by data sensitivity and migration cost
boundary: breaks down if a practical quantum computer arrives before migration infrastructure reaches critical mass

name: Secret Sharing Modularity Resilience Theory
variation: threshold scheme parameters (k-of-n), share holder reliability, and geographic distribution patterns
selection: share loss and compromise events test whether reconstruction threshold is still achievable
fitness: maximized secret availability while minimizing probability of unauthorized reconstruction
boundary: breaks down when correlated failures violate share independence assumptions

name: Mental Accounting Compartmentalization
variation: framing of gains/losses across categories
selection: cognitive ease and emotional comfort in financial decisions
fitness: perceived utility maximized per mental bucket
boundary: breaks when fungibility of money is recognized or stakes are extreme

name: Hyperbolic Discounting Drift
variation: delay length and reward magnitude framing
selection: present-bias overwhelms long-term planning
fitness: immediate reward capture rate
boundary: collapses when commitment devices eliminate future choice revision

name: Loss Aversion Asymmetry
variation: endowment shifts and reference point anchoring
selection: asymmetric weighting of losses over equivalent gains
fitness: minimized perceived loss relative to reference point
boundary: fails in experienced traders and when stakes are trivially small

name: Herd Cascade Formation
unit: individual decision in a social sequence
variation: private signal quality and observation order
selection: rational imitation dominates when private info is weak
fitness: social conformity payoff minus information loss
boundary: shatters when a credible public signal contradicts the cascade

name: Nudge Architecture Evolution
variation: default option design, ordering, and salience manipulation
selection: opt-out friction filters less motivated agents
fitness: target behavior adoption rate
boundary: erodes when decision-makers become aware of manipulation

name: Sunk Cost Escalation Trap
variation: magnitude of prior investment and visibility of losses
selection: psychological need to justify past expenditure
fitness: minimized cognitive dissonance from abandonment
boundary: dissolves when external accountability forces rational exit

name: Anchoring Propagation in Valuation
variation: anchor magnitude, relevance, and presentation order
selection: insufficient adjustment from salient starting value
fitness: estimate precision relative to true value
boundary: weakens with domain expertise and deliberate debiasing protocols

name: Endowment Effect Stickiness
variation: ownership duration, emotional attachment, and market liquidity
selection: ownership inflates subjective value above market clearing price
fitness: willingness-to-accept / willingness-to-pay gap
boundary: collapses for fungible goods traded by experienced market participants

name: Prospect Theory Editing Phase
variation: outcome segregation, combination, and cancellation heuristics
selection: simplification that preserves decision-relevant contrasts
fitness: cognitive load reduction without reversing preference ordering
boundary: fails when editing operations interact to produce intransitive choices

name: Soil Aggregate Hierarchy Formation
variation: organic matter input, clay mineralogy, and microbial exudate diversity
selection: wetting-drying and freeze-thaw cycles destroy weak aggregates
fitness: aggregate stability index (resistance to slaking)
boundary: breaks in saturated conditions where matric forces vanish

name: Rhizosphere Carbon Pump
variation: plant species, root architecture, and exudate chemistry
selection: microbial competition for labile carbon near root surfaces
fitness: microbial biomass carbon turnover efficiency
boundary: collapses in nutrient-saturated soils where exudate priming is negligible

name: Pedogenic Threshold Switching
variation: parent material composition and climate regime shifts
selection: weathering rate exceeds mineral buffering capacity
fitness: horizon differentiation intensity
boundary: invalid in young alluvial deposits with insufficient time for profile development

name: Mycorrhizal Nutrient Exchange Network
variation: fungal species diversity and host plant root density
selection: reciprocal reward: plants starve non-cooperative fungi of carbon
fitness: phosphorus transfer efficiency per unit carbon invested
boundary: collapses under high soil phosphorus where mycorrhizal dependency vanishes

name: Soil Organic Matter Saturation Deficit
variation: clay surface area, iron oxide content, and organic input quality
selection: sorption affinity preferentially stabilizes low-molecular-weight compounds
fitness: carbon saturation ratio (actual / capacity)
boundary: meaningless in organic soils (histosols) where mineral surfaces are negligible

name: Redox Oscillation Nutrient Cycling
variation: water table fluctuation amplitude and organic electron donor supply
selection: thermodynamic favorability of terminal electron acceptors
fitness: free energy yield per electron transferred
boundary: ceases in permanently aerated or permanently waterlogged conditions

name: Soil Macropore Preferential Flow
variation: bioturbation intensity, root channels, and shrink-swell clay content
selection: gravity and matric potential gradients route water through lowest-resistance paths
fitness: hydraulic conductivity per unit cross-section
boundary: irrelevant in coarse sandy soils where matrix flow dominates

name: Cation Exchange Buffering Cascade
unit: exchange site on clay or humus colloid
variation: clay type, organic matter charge density, and ionic strength of soil solution
selection: valence and hydration radius determine exchange selectivity
fitness: buffering capacity (resistance to pH change per unit acid added)
boundary: overwhelmed by acid deposition rates exceeding exchange replenishment

name: Biological Soil Crust Succession
variation: propagule dispersal, substrate texture, and disturbance history
selection: desiccation tolerance and UV resistance filter colonizers
fitness: surface coverage persistence through disturbance cycles
boundary: destroyed by mechanical trampling or dense vascular plant canopy closure

name: Scale-Free Hub Vulnerability
variation: preferential attachment strength and degree distribution exponent
selection: targeted attack removes highest-degree nodes first
fitness: network connectivity (giant component size) post-attack
boundary: irrelevant in homogeneous random graphs lacking degree heterogeneity

name: Small-World Rewiring Dynamics
variation: rewiring probability interpolates lattice to random graph
selection: path-length reduction per added shortcut
fitness: clustering coefficient / average path length ratio
boundary: vanishes when rewiring probability approaches 1 and clustering collapses

name: Community Detection Resolution Limit
variation: modularity resolution parameter and network size
selection: optimization algorithms merge small communities below resolution threshold
fitness: modularity score Q at given resolution
boundary: fails to detect communities smaller than sqrt(2m) where m is edge count

name: Epidemic Threshold on Contact Networks
variation: contact network topology and transmission/recovery rate ratio
selection: degree heterogeneity lowers epidemic threshold toward zero
fitness: basic reproduction number R0 on the network
boundary: invalid when contact patterns are temporal and non-Markovian

name: Structural Controllability of Directed Networks
variation: degree distribution and degree correlations in directed edges
selection: maximum matching determines minimum driver node set
fitness: fraction of nodes that must be directly controlled
boundary: breaks when edge weights are dynamic or nonlinear dynamics dominate

name: Network Motif Enrichment Selection
variation: functional requirements generate non-random subgraph frequencies
selection: motifs enabling stable dynamics are overrepresented versus null model
fitness: motif z-score (enrichment significance)
boundary: loses meaning in dense networks where all subgraph counts converge

name: Link Prediction via Structural Similarity
variation: common neighbors, path length, and community co-membership
selection: similarity scores rank unobserved edges by formation likelihood
fitness: AUC of predicted versus actual future links
boundary: fails in networks with strong weak-tie formation or adversarial edge creation

name: Multiplex Layer Interdependence Cascade
variation: layer overlap, coupling strength, and node multiplexity
selection: failure in one layer propagates through dependency links to others
fitness: mutual giant component size under cascading failure
boundary: reduces to single-network percolation when layers are fully correlated

name: Network Embeddedness and Trust Formation
variation: structural embeddedness (shared partners) and relational history
selection: reputation cost of defection scales with mutual connections
fitness: cooperation rate in embedded versus arm's-length transactions
boundary: ineffective in anonymous large-scale markets with low repeat interaction

name: Aristotelian Ethos-Pathos-Logos Triangle
variation: Speakers vary the balance of credibility (ethos), emotional resonance (pathos), and logical structure (logos) across arguments, producing a combinatorial space of persuasive configurations.
selection: Audiences selectively retain and act upon arguments whose appeal-mix matches their cognitive and emotional receptivity.
fitness: A rhetorical appeal achieves fitness when its triangular balance produces attitude change or action in the target audience.
boundary: The triangle collapses when audiences lack shared epistemic standards, when speaker identity is unknown, or when emotional saturation renders pathos indistinguishable from noise.

name: Chiasmus Structural Inversion
variation: Rhetorical structures vary by degree of mirror symmetry (AB-BA), depth of nesting, and semantic contrast between inverted elements.
selection: Chiastic patterns that produce memorable cognitive tension are preferentially quoted, repeated, and adapted; flat inversions are forgotten.
fitness: A chiasmus achieves fitness when its structural inversion encodes a conceptual insight that listeners perceive as simultaneously surprising and inevitable.
boundary: Breaks down when inverted elements lack genuine semantic tension or when the structure exceeds working memory capacity.

name: Enthymeme Suppressed Premise
variation: Arguments vary in which premise is suppressed and the cognitive distance required for audience reconstruction.
selection: Enthymemes whose suppressed premises match audience prior beliefs are experienced as compelling and self-evident.
fitness: An enthymeme achieves fitness when the suppressed premise is exactly recoverable by the target audience, creating co-authorship.
boundary: Fails when audiences lack the background knowledge to reconstruct the missing premise or when multiple incompatible premises could fill the gap.

name: Kairos Timeliness in Rhetoric
variation: The same argument delivered at different moments encounters different audience states, institutional contexts, and competing narratives.
selection: Arguments launched at kairotic moments achieve disproportionate uptake; identical arguments at non-kairotic moments dissipate.
fitness: A kairotic intervention achieves fitness when temporal alignment produces a phase transition in audience belief or institutional action.
boundary: Kairos becomes indeterminate when multiple competing moments appear equally opportune or when manufactured urgency erodes genuine temporal sensitivity.

name: Burkean Identification
variation: Speakers create identification through varied strategies: shared terminology, common enemies, overlapping interests, stylistic mimicry.
selection: Identification strategies that bridge genuine divisions while maintaining authentic distinctness persist; manipulative ones provoke counter-identification.
fitness: Identification achieves fitness when symbolic alignment produces cooperative action between previously divided groups.
boundary: Fails when material interests diverge so sharply that no symbolic bridge can sustain identification.

name: Stasis Theory Issue Framing
variation: Disputes can be framed at the level of fact, definition, quality, or jurisdiction, producing fundamentally different argumentative terrains.
selection: The stasis level where genuine disagreement concentrates becomes the productive locus of debate.
fitness: Stasis framing achieves fitness when it locates the actual point of controversy, enabling productive clash.
boundary: Breaks down when disputants cannot agree on which stasis level is operative or when power asymmetries impose a stasis level.

name: Amplification Through Accumulatio
variation: Speakers vary the number, specificity, emotional register, and sequencing of accumulated details.
selection: Accumulations that build toward a perceptual threshold persist; lists that merely enumerate are pruned.
fitness: Accumulatio achieves fitness when aggregation produces an emergent impression greater than the sum of individual items.
boundary: Fails when accumulated details are too heterogeneous to cohere or when audience attention exhausts before threshold.

name: Epideictic Praise-Blame Rhetoric
variation: Epideictic speeches vary along the praise-blame axis, in choice of exemplary figures, and in values elevated as communal standards.
selection: Communities selectively preserve epideictic performances that reinforce or productively challenge shared values.
fitness: An epideictic performance achieves fitness when it strengthens communal value coherence.
boundary: Collapses when communities lack sufficient value consensus for praise to resonate or blame to sting.

name: Ciceronian Five-Canon System
variation: Each canon (inventio, dispositio, elocutio, memoria, actio) admits internal variation; speakers differentially allocate effort across canons.
selection: Performances where canon allocation matches situational demands outperform misallocated efforts.
fitness: The system achieves fitness when sequential processing through all five canons produces persuasive impact exceeding any subset alone.
boundary: Fails in improvisational contexts where invention and delivery collapse into simultaneity.

name: Laban Effort-Shape Movement Analysis
variation: Movers vary across four effort factors (weight, space, time, flow) and shape dimensions, generating a high-dimensional space of qualitative movement possibilities.
selection: Choreographers and audiences select movement qualities that produce legible expressive states.
fitness: A movement quality achieves fitness when its effort-shape configuration communicates intended meaning with minimal ambiguity.
boundary: Loses discriminatory power at movement extremes or in culturally specific gesture systems where Laban categories misalign.

name: Contact Improvisation Weight Sharing
variation: Partners vary in the amount of weight given and received, body surfaces used, speed of transfer, and degree of mutual support.
selection: Weight-sharing configurations maintaining mutual safety while enabling surprising movement are sustained; risky ones are abandoned.
fitness: A weight exchange achieves fitness when both partners access positions and transitions unavailable to either alone.
boundary: Fails when mass differential exceeds meaningful weight sharing range or when trust is insufficient.

name: Ensemble Unison-Canon Timing
variation: Ensemble members vary in temporal offset from a reference phrase, from perfect unison through staggered canon to fully independent timing.
selection: Timing patterns producing visually coherent effects are retained; ambiguous patterns read as error and are corrected.
fitness: A timing pattern achieves fitness when the temporal relationship communicates structural intention without ambiguity.
boundary: Breaks down when dancer count exceeds audience tracking capacity or when spatial separation prevents timing reference.

name: Forsythe Improvisation Technologies
variation: Dancers apply varied algorithmic operations (point operations, line extrusions, geometric transformations) producing different movement outcomes.
selection: Operations generating high novelty and structural coherence are retained; repetitive or unsustainable ones are abandoned.
fitness: An improvisation technology achieves fitness when its constraint generates a larger and more diverse movement vocabulary than unconstrained improvisation.
boundary: Collapses when dancers internalize operations so thoroughly that output becomes predictable.

name: Choreographic Phrase Development
variation: A seed phrase is developed through retrograde, fragmentation, augmentation, diminution, reordering, and qualitative transformation.
selection: Developed phrases maintaining recognizable kinship with the seed while introducing novelty are retained.
fitness: Phrase development achieves fitness when the family of related phrases creates thematic coherence across a work.
boundary: Breaks down when the seed phrase lacks internal structure or when too many simultaneous operations obscure the relationship.

name: Site-Specific Movement Adaptation
variation: Choreographic material transforms as it encounters varied architectural features, surfaces, sightlines, and social contexts.
selection: Adaptations revealing unique site properties are retained; those merely transplanting theater conventions are rejected.
fitness: Site-specific adaptation achieves fitness when the encounter between movement and environment produces meaning unavailable in either alone.
boundary: Fails when sites are too dangerous or when regulatory constraints prevent genuine physical engagement.

name: Release Technique Energy Conservation
variation: Dancers vary which muscle groups release, timing and speed, degree of surrender to gravity, and skeletal alignment.
selection: Release patterns efficiently converting potential to kinetic energy are cultivated; inefficient ones are corrected.
fitness: A release event achieves fitness when minimal muscular effort produces maximal movement result.
boundary: Fails when insufficient core stability makes full release dangerous.

name: Cunningham Chance-Based Composition
variation: Choreographic elements independently determined by chance operations, producing combinations no aesthetic preference would generate.
selection: Physical impossibility and performer capacity impose selection during rehearsal; unperformable outcomes are modified.
fitness: A chance-based composition achieves fitness when removal of choreographic habit reveals aesthetic possibilities invisible to intentional composition.
boundary: Collapses when performers unconsciously smooth chance-generated sequences into habitual patterns.

name: Floor Pattern Spatial Design
variation: Dancers vary trajectories (linear, circular, diagonal, spiral) and spatial relationships (clustering, distribution, asymmetric grouping).
selection: Floor patterns creating legible spatial narratives are preserved; those producing visual clutter are revised.
fitness: A spatial design achieves fitness when floor patterns reinforce choreographic meaning and sustain visual interest.
boundary: Fails in venues where sightlines prevent perception of floor patterns or when dancer count is insufficient.

name: Steel Tempering Martensite Transformation
variation: Rapid quenching traps carbon in a body-centered tetragonal lattice; martensite varies in carbon concentration, plate size, and twin density.
selection: Tempering temperature selects which martensite substructures decompose: low temperatures relieve stress while preserving hardness.
fitness: A tempered martensite microstructure achieves fitness when it delivers the target combination of hardness, toughness, and fatigue resistance.
boundary: Fails when carbon content is insufficient to form martensite or when service temperatures exceed tempering temperature.

name: Alloy Phase Diagram Eutectic Points
variation: Binary and multicomponent alloys vary in composition across phase diagrams, producing different solidification paths and microstructural morphologies.
selection: Eutectic compositions are naturally selected in casting because their single-temperature solidification eliminates the mushy zone.
fitness: A eutectic alloy achieves fitness when its lamellar microstructure provides optimal castability, mechanical properties, and cost.
boundary: Phase diagrams lose predictive power when cooling rates deviate far from equilibrium assumptions.

name: Grain Boundary Strengthening (Hall-Petch)
variation: Polycrystalline metals vary in grain size from millimeters to nanometers, with each boundary presenting different misorientation and character.
selection: Processing routes producing finer grain sizes are selected for structural applications because Hall-Petch delivers higher yield strength.
fitness: Grain boundary strengthening achieves fitness when the inverse-square-root relationship delivers required properties without sacrificing ductility.
boundary: The Hall-Petch relationship inverts below approximately 10-20 nanometers (inverse Hall-Petch).

name: Work Hardening Dislocation Accumulation
variation: Plastic deformation generates dislocations varying in type, density, spatial distribution, and interaction strength.
selection: Dislocation configurations minimizing total elastic strain energy are thermodynamically favored, driving self-organization.
fitness: Work hardening achieves fitness when accumulated dislocation density raises flow stress sufficiently to distribute deformation uniformly.
boundary: Fails at high temperatures where thermal activation enables annihilation faster than accumulation.

name: Galvanic Corrosion Potential Difference
variation: When dissimilar metals contact in an electrolyte, potential difference varies with alloy composition, surface condition, and electrolyte chemistry.
selection: The more anodic metal is selectively corroded, sacrificing material to protect the cathodic partner.
fitness: A galvanic system achieves engineering fitness when deliberately exploited for cathodic protection.
boundary: Breaks down when electrolyte conductivity is too low or when passive films decouple behavior from thermodynamic predictions.

name: Powder Metallurgy Sintering
variation: Metal powder compacts vary in particle size distribution, morphology, composition, and packing geometry.
selection: Sintering selectively eliminates high-energy features: small pores shrink, small particles are consumed (Ostwald ripening).
fitness: A sintered part achieves fitness when neck growth produces sufficient mechanical integrity while maintaining dimensional control.
boundary: Fails when surface contamination prevents neck formation or when trapped gas resists final densification.

name: Shape Memory Alloy Phase Transitions
variation: Shape memory alloys vary in transformation temperatures, hysteresis width, recoverable strain, and martensite variants formed.
selection: Thermomechanical cycling selects for martensite variants accommodating applied strain most efficiently.
fitness: A shape memory alloy achieves fitness when reversible martensitic transformation reliably converts thermal input to mechanical output.
boundary: Fails when operating temperatures fall outside the transformation window or when cyclic fatigue accumulates irrecoverable strain.

name: Precipitation Hardening (Age Hardening)
variation: Supersaturated solid solutions decompose to form precipitates varying in composition, crystal structure, size, shape, and coherency.
selection: Aging time and temperature select the precipitate population: underaging produces shearable GP zones; overaging produces bypassable particles.
fitness: Precipitation hardening achieves fitness at peak age, when precipitate size and spacing maximize dislocation resistance.
boundary: Fails when solubility limits prevent supersaturation or when service temperatures cause continued aging past peak.

name: Zone Refining Purification
variation: A narrow molten zone traverses a solid ingot; impurities vary in segregation coefficient producing different redistribution profiles.
selection: Impurities with segregation coefficients less than one are swept to one end; those greater than one concentrate oppositely.
fitness: Zone refining achieves fitness when multiple passes produce material of sufficient purity for the application.
boundary: Approaches theoretical limits when segregation coefficients are near unity or when solid-state diffusion homogenizes between passes.

name: Archard's Adhesive Wear Law
variation: surface roughness, load distribution, sliding speed
selection: junctions that exceed shear strength transfer material
fitness: wear volume proportional to load and sliding distance
boundary: hardness of softer surface, lubrication regime, temperature

name: Stribeck Curve Transitions
variation: viscosity, speed, and load shift the film thickness ratio
selection: regime with lowest friction coefficient dominates performance
fitness: minimized friction coefficient at operating point
boundary: fluid viscosity limits, surface roughness, thermal degradation

name: Fretting Fatigue Crack Nucleation
variation: oscillatory micro-slip amplitude and contact stress distribution
selection: cracks nucleate where tangential stress exceeds fatigue limit
fitness: crack propagation rate under combined fretting and bulk stress
boundary: slip amplitude threshold, material fatigue limit, oxidation rate

name: Triboelectric Charge Separation
variation: contact area, material work-function mismatch, humidity
selection: charge polarity determined by electron affinity difference
fitness: net surface charge density after separation
boundary: humidity dissipation, dielectric breakdown, surface contamination

name: Third-Body Layer Formation
unit: wear debris particle trapped in contact
variation: debris composition, size, and recirculation within the contact zone
selection: stable third bodies reduce direct surface contact and wear
fitness: longevity of protective debris layer under sustained load
boundary: ejection rate, oxidation, load-induced compaction limits

name: Hydraulic Jump Energy Dissipation
variation: upstream Froude number, channel geometry, tailwater depth
selection: flow transitions from supercritical to subcritical at momentum balance
fitness: energy head loss across the jump
boundary: Froude number threshold, channel width, downstream conditions

name: Dam Overtopping Failure Cascade
variation: inflow hydrograph shape, spillway capacity, reservoir volume
selection: erosion accelerates where overtopping velocity exceeds soil resistance
fitness: time to breach formation (inverse = risk)
boundary: spillway design capacity, embankment material cohesion, flood duration

name: Hardy-Cross Pipe Network Balancing
unit: flow loop in a pipe network
variation: demand node draws, pipe diameters, roughness coefficients
selection: iterative correction drives loop head-loss residuals toward zero
fitness: minimized pressure residual across all loops
boundary: pipe capacity, minimum service pressure, network topology

name: Shields Sediment Entrainment Threshold
variation: grain size distribution, bed shear stress, channel slope
selection: grains mobilize when shear stress exceeds critical Shields parameter
fitness: sediment transport rate above threshold
boundary: critical shear stress, grain packing, armoring of bed surface

name: Water Hammer Pressure Transient
unit: pressure wave front in a closed conduit
variation: valve closure rate, pipe elasticity, flow velocity
selection: wave amplitude determined by Joukowsky equation at reflection points
fitness: peak transient pressure relative to pipe burst rating
boundary: pipe wall elasticity, fluid compressibility, system damping

name: Benford's Law Digit Frequency Anomaly
unit: leading digit of a financial entry
variation: naturally occurring vs. fabricated number distributions
selection: deviations from expected log-distribution flag manipulation
fitness: chi-squared divergence from Benford's expected frequencies
boundary: sample size, data type suitability, legitimate uniform-range data

name: Round-Number Clustering in Fraud
variation: legitimate expense rounding vs. fabricated round figures
selection: excess clustering at round numbers signals cognitive bias in fraud
fitness: ratio of round-number transactions to expected baseline
boundary: industry norms, pricing conventions, approval thresholds

name: Temporal Gap Analysis in Audit Trails
unit: time interval between sequential ledger entries
variation: normal posting rhythm vs. gaps indicating deleted or backdated entries
selection: statistically anomalous gaps or timestamp clusters indicate tampering
fitness: deviation of inter-entry intervals from expected Poisson process
boundary: system clock precision, batch posting schedules, time-zone artifacts

name: Shell Company Network Detection
unit: corporate entity node in ownership graph
variation: layering depth, shared directors, circular ownership structures
selection: unusual graph centrality or cyclicity flags concealment structures
fitness: opacity score — ratio of beneficial ownership hops to transaction volume
boundary: jurisdictional disclosure requirements, data availability, nominee masking

name: Duplicate Payment Entropy Analysis
variation: field similarity across vendor, amount, date, and invoice number
selection: near-duplicate pairs with minor field mutations flag intentional splitting
fitness: Jaccard similarity score between flagged payment pairs
boundary: legitimate recurring payments, multi-currency rounding, system migrations

name: Ant Colony Pheromone Trail Optimization
variation: random path exploration by individual ants
selection: shorter paths accumulate pheromone faster due to higher traversal rate
fitness: pheromone concentration as proxy for path quality
boundary: evaporation rate, colony size, environment change rate

name: Reynolds Flocking Alignment Rule
variation: local velocity differences among neighbors within perception radius
selection: agents that align with local average heading reduce collision risk
fitness: group coherence measured by velocity correlation
boundary: perception radius, agent density, obstacle density, noise

name: Quorum Sensing Activation Threshold
variation: individual emission rate, diffusion, population density
selection: collective behavior activates only above concentration threshold
fitness: coordination benefit minus signaling cost
boundary: diffusion rate, receptor sensitivity, environmental dilution

name: Particle Swarm Velocity Update
variation: stochastic weighting of personal best, global best, and inertia
selection: particles converge toward regions of improving objective value
fitness: objective function value at particle position
boundary: inertia weight, velocity clamp, search space dimensionality

name: Honeybee Waggle Dance Recruitment
variation: dance duration and angle encode distance and direction to food source
selection: richer sources recruit more foragers via longer dances
fitness: colony energy intake rate from recruited foraging
boundary: dance floor congestion, source depletion rate, predation risk

name: Paris Law Fatigue Crack Growth
variation: cyclic stress amplitude, load ratio, crack length
selection: cracks grow when stress intensity range exceeds threshold
fitness: crack growth rate per cycle (da/dN)
boundary: fracture toughness, stress intensity threshold, environment

name: Stress Corrosion Cracking Synergy
unit: crack tip in corrosive environment under tensile stress
variation: alloy susceptibility, corrodent concentration, stress level
selection: crack advances where chemical attack and stress concentrate together
fitness: crack velocity under combined mechanical and chemical driving force
boundary: threshold stress intensity, passivation potential, temperature

name: Larson-Miller Creep Rupture Prediction
variation: temperature, applied stress, microstructural variability
selection: void coalescence along grain boundaries leads to intergranular fracture
fitness: time-to-rupture at given temperature and stress
boundary: melting point fraction, grain size, oxidation environment

name: Ductile-Brittle Transition Temperature
variation: temperature, strain rate, notch geometry, microstructure
selection: below transition temperature, cleavage fracture dominates over yielding
fitness: absorbed impact energy (Charpy value)
boundary: transition temperature range, grain size, alloy composition

name: Bathtub Curve Failure Rate Evolution
variation: manufacturing defects, random operational stress, wear-out mechanisms
selection: early failures removed by burn-in; wear-out failures cluster at end of life
fitness: instantaneous hazard rate at operating time
boundary: infant mortality screening, design life, maintenance intervention

name: Plant Guild Mutualistic Assembly
variation: root depth, canopy height, nitrogen fixation, pest deterrence
selection: guilds that maximize niche complementarity outperform monocultures
fitness: total guild yield per unit area and external input
boundary: climate zone, soil type, water availability, guild complexity limits

name: Zone-Based Attention Gradient
variation: distance from dwelling, visit frequency, intervention intensity
selection: high-maintenance elements placed in high-attention zones
fitness: labor efficiency — yield per unit of human transit energy
boundary: site topography, access paths, climate extremes, land area

name: Keyline Water Harvesting Cascade
variation: swale spacing, depth, keyline angle, soil permeability
selection: water slows and infiltrates at each level, reducing runoff and erosion
fitness: soil moisture retention and aquifer recharge rate
boundary: rainfall intensity, slope grade, soil infiltration capacity, frost line

name: Closed-Loop Nutrient Cycling
unit: nutrient atom (N, P, K) in the farm system
variation: composting method, animal integration, cover crop selection
selection: systems minimizing nutrient export maintain long-term soil fertility
fitness: nutrient balance ratio — internal cycling vs. external inputs
boundary: harvest removal, leaching loss, biological fixation capacity

name: Edge Effect Productivity Amplification
variation: edge geometry (straight, crenulated, spiral), adjacent biome types
selection: edges with greater interface length access more niches and resources
fitness: species diversity and yield per unit area along edge
boundary: minimum patch size, microclimate extremes, management complexity

name: Phoneme Neutralization
variation: allophonic variation across prosodic positions
selection: contrasts lost in weak positions, preserved in strong positions
fitness: perceptual distinctiveness under articulatory ease
boundary: syllable structure, stress placement, speech rate

name: Tonal Register Drift
variation: pitch range shifts across generations of speakers
selection: listener discrimination pressure maintains separation between tone categories
fitness: functional load of tonal contrasts in the lexicon
boundary: pitch range of the vocal tract, number of tonal categories, speech community size

name: Vowel Chain Shift
variation: incremental articulatory drift in vowel targets
selection: movement of one vowel pushes or pulls neighbors to maintain perceptual distance
fitness: maximal dispersion across available acoustic space
boundary: articulatory limits, number of vowel contrasts, formant frequency ranges

name: Prosodic Bootstrapping
variation: infant exposure to variable pitch, duration, and pause patterns
selection: cues that reliably predict syntactic boundaries are weighted higher
fitness: accuracy of word and phrase segmentation from speech stream
boundary: input frequency, ambient language typology, maturational timing

name: Sonority Sequencing Principle
variation: cross-linguistic differences in permissible consonant clusters
selection: clusters violating sonority rise are disfavored or repaired by epenthesis
fitness: perceptual recoverability of segment sequences
boundary: language-specific sonority scale, morphological pressure, loanword adaptation

name: Fitts's Law Motor Targeting
variation: speed-accuracy tradeoff across target distances and widths
selection: movement strategies that minimize time for a required accuracy level
fitness: throughput (bits per second of motor information)
boundary: target size, distance, limb biomechanics, device input resolution

name: Cognitive Load Partitioning
unit: chunk of task information in working memory
variation: different distributions of intrinsic, extraneous, and germane load
selection: designs that reduce extraneous load free capacity for germane processing
fitness: learning gain or task accuracy per unit of mental effort
boundary: working memory capacity (~4 chunks), task complexity, expertise level

name: Alarm Fatigue Habituation
unit: operator response to an alarm signal
variation: alarm frequency, false-positive rate, severity coding
selection: high false-alarm rates depress response probability through habituation
fitness: correct response rate to true-positive alarms
boundary: base rate of real events, alarm discriminability, operator workload

name: Affordance-Control Mapping
variation: spatial, semantic, and cultural mapping conventions
selection: mappings congruent with user mental models reduce errors
fitness: error rate and response latency on first use
boundary: cultural conventions, physical constraints, number of controls

name: Situation Awareness Degradation
unit: operator mental model of system state
variation: automation level, display update rate, task interruptions
selection: out-of-the-loop operators lose state tracking under high automation
fitness: accuracy of operator predictions about near-future system behavior
boundary: automation transparency, time pressure, task complexity, alerting design

name: Intercropping Resource Complementarity
unit: crop species pair in a polyculture
variation: root depth stratification, canopy architecture, nutrient demand profiles
selection: species combinations that partition niche space outperform monocultures
fitness: land equivalent ratio (LER > 1)
boundary: soil type, water availability, planting density, allelopathy

name: Plant-Soil Microbiome Feedback
variation: root exudate composition shapes rhizosphere assembly
selection: beneficial microbe consortia enhance host nutrient uptake; pathogens accumulate under monoculture
fitness: net plant growth response to conditioned soil
boundary: crop rotation length, soil organic matter, tillage intensity

name: Pest Dilution Effect
unit: herbivore-host encounter in a diverse field
variation: crop diversity, spatial arrangement, non-host plant density
selection: specialist herbivores have lower colonization success in diverse stands
fitness: inverse pest load per unit crop yield
boundary: pest mobility, host specificity, field scale, natural enemy abundance

name: Nutrient Cycling Closure
unit: nutrient atom (N, P, K) in the farm system
variation: cover crop species, residue management, composting method
selection: systems with tighter nutrient loops reduce input dependency
fitness: ratio of internally recycled to externally imported nutrients
boundary: leaching rate, harvest export, soil buffering capacity, climate

name: Agroforestry Microclimate Buffering
unit: understory crop zone beneath tree canopy
variation: tree species, spacing, pruning regime, canopy density
selection: shade and windbreak effects buffer temperature and moisture extremes
fitness: yield stability across variable climate years
boundary: light competition threshold, root zone overlap, latitude, rainfall regime

name: Ice Core Isotope Thermometry
unit: annual ice layer with delta-18O / delta-D signature
variation: isotopic fractionation varies with condensation temperature
selection: heavier isotopes preferentially rain out at warmer temperatures
fitness: correlation strength between isotope ratio and independent temperature estimates
boundary: ice flow distortion, diffusion smoothing, elevation changes, seasonality bias

name: Dansgaard-Oeschger Oscillation
unit: abrupt warming event in glacial climate
variation: freshwater forcing, sea-ice extent, thermohaline circulation strength
selection: system bistability causes rapid flips between stadial and interstadial states
fitness: amplitude and regularity of ~1470-year temperature swings
boundary: ice sheet volume, ocean salt budget, orbital forcing baseline

name: Tree-Ring Crossdating Chronology
unit: annual tree ring width in a cross-dated network
variation: growth response to precipitation, temperature, and disturbance varies by site
selection: climate signal emerges from averaging over many trees; non-climatic noise cancels
fitness: expressed population signal (EPS) of the chronology
boundary: series length, sample depth, biological growth trends, site homogeneity

name: Milankovitch Orbital Forcing
variation: eccentricity (~100 kyr), obliquity (~41 kyr), precession (~23 kyr) cycles
selection: ice sheets grow when summer insolation at 65N falls below ablation threshold
fitness: correlation between orbital parameters and ice volume proxy (benthic delta-18O)
boundary: CO2 feedback strength, ice albedo, ocean heat transport, continental configuration

name: Volcanic Sulfate Forcing
unit: stratospheric sulfate aerosol loading from eruption
variation: eruption magnitude, latitude, season, sulfur yield
selection: large tropical eruptions produce global cooling; high-latitude eruptions have regional effect
fitness: magnitude and duration of post-eruption temperature anomaly
boundary: background aerosol level, climate sensitivity, ocean thermal inertia, coincident forcing

name: Formulaic Speech Preservation
unit: formulaic phrase or epithet in oral performance
variation: singer substitutes formulas within metrical slots
selection: metrically fitting, semantically apt formulas are retained across generations
fitness: transmission fidelity of narrative content over retellings
boundary: metrical system constraints, performer memory, audience expectations, genre rules

name: Serial Reproduction Drift
unit: narrative element in a transmission chain
variation: reconstructive memory errors at each retelling
selection: schema-consistent elements survive; counter-intuitive details are lost or transformed
fitness: memorability and cultural relevance of narrative elements
boundary: chain length, retelling interval, cultural schema, performer skill

name: Mnemonic Scaffolding in Oral Epics
unit: structural episode in an epic narrative
variation: ring composition, parallelism, and spatial memory palace techniques
selection: structurally scaffolded sequences resist forgetting better than linear lists
fitness: proportion of canonical episodes recalled across performers
boundary: epic length, performer training duration, performance frequency, audience correction

name: Minimal Counterintuitiveness Effect
variation: number and type of ontological violations per narrative
selection: narratives with a small number of counterintuitive elements are recalled and transmitted best
fitness: recall advantage and cultural spread rate
boundary: cognitive load, cultural familiarity, number of violations per concept, narrative length

name: Performance-Audience Co-Selection
variation: audience reactions (attention, laughter, requests) shape performer emphasis
selection: episodes and embellishments that elicit strong audience response are amplified
fitness: audience engagement and invitation rate for the performer
boundary: audience size, cultural norms of participation, performance context, repertoire breadth

name: Inflammation Resolution Cascade
variation: timing and magnitude of omega-3-derived mediator synthesis
selection: active resolution signals switch macrophages from M1 to M2 phenotype
fitness: speed of neutrophil clearance and transition to proliferative phase

variation: extracellular matrix stiffness, TGF-beta concentration, cell contractility
selection: stiff matrix sustains myofibroblast activation; soft matrix triggers apoptosis
fitness: wound contraction rate balanced against fibrosis risk

name: Angiogenic Sprouting Gradient
unit: endothelial tip cell at a vascular sprout
variation: VEGF gradient steepness, Notch-Delta lateral inhibition among endothelial cells
selection: tip cells with highest VEGF receptor density lead sprouts; stalk cells proliferate behind
fitness: vascular density sufficient to oxygenate granulation tissue

unit: keratinocyte at the wound edge migration front
variation: leader cell selection, contact inhibition release, EGF/KGF signaling
selection: coordinated sheet migration closes the wound faster than individual cell crawling
fitness: time to complete epithelial barrier restoration

unit: collagen fiber in the remodeling scar

selection: fibers aligned with principal stress vectors resist degradation; misaligned fibers are cleaved

variation: bacterial strain diversity and temperature fluctuation during secondary fermentation
selection: lactic acid bacteria outcompete spoilage organisms under controlled pH descent

variation: geological heterogeneity in bedrock composition and water table depth
selection: root systems preferentially exploit mineral-rich fracture zones

name: Tannin Polymerization Cascade
