selection: folding regimes that homogenize carbon without excessive decarburization produce reliable blades
fitness: uniformity of carbon distribution across cross-section
boundary: forge temperature limits, oxidation loss per fold, diminishing returns past ~15 folds

name: Hexagonal Comb Tiling Efficiency
unit: wax cell in the comb lattice
variation: cell diameter, wall thickness, tilt angle from horizontal
selection: cell geometries that maximize storage volume per unit wax are built by colonies with higher honey surplus
fitness: volume-to-wax-mass ratio of the comb structure
boundary: wax secretion energy cost, larval size constraints, structural load from stored honey

name: Propolis Envelope Sealing Strategy
unit: propolis deposit at a gap or crack in the hive
variation: resin source plant species, application thickness, coverage extent
selection: colonies with thorough propolis envelopes show lower pathogen loads and higher overwinter survival
fitness: inverse colony pathogen load times overwinter survival rate
boundary: resin availability in foraging range, wax-propolis energy trade-off, hive entrance size

name: Ventilation Corridor Thermoregulation
unit: fanning bee position and airflow corridor
variation: number of fanning bees, corridor width, entrance orientation
selection: ventilation patterns that maintain brood nest at 34-36°C with minimal fanning workforce are favoured
fitness: brood nest temperature stability (inverse variance) per fanning bee deployed
boundary: ambient temperature range, colony population, hive insulation, humidity tolerance

name: Honey Storage Spatial Gradient
unit: comb region allocated to honey, pollen, or brood
variation: distance from brood nest, frame position, nectar flow intensity
selection: spatial arrangements that place honey above and beside brood with pollen interface optimize nurse bee access
fitness: brood-rearing rate per unit foraging effort
boundary: comb area, colony size, seasonal nectar availability, queen laying rate

name: Swarm Comb Construction Consensus
unit: initial comb tongue built by a newly hived swarm
variation: festooning chain length, wax scale production rate, ambient temperature
selection: colonies that rapidly build parallel combs with correct bee-space gaps establish faster and survive
fitness: comb area built per day in the first week times structural regularity
boundary: available wax budget from gorged workers, cavity volume, ambient temperature minimum for wax plasticity

name: Complication Interaction Coupling
variation: gear ratios, lever geometries, and spring tensions differ across complication designs
selection: configurations where multiple complications share a motion train without interference persist in haute horlogerie
fitness: number of simultaneous complications that operate without positional error accumulation
boundary: available movement volume, pivot friction limits, hand-setting torque budget

name: Lubrication Degradation Cycle
unit: oil film at a pivot jewel interface
variation: lubricant viscosity, jewel surface energy, capillary geometry at each pivot
selection: pivot points where oil migration or oxidation causes amplitude loss are identified at service intervals
fitness: time in years before amplitude drops below isochronous threshold
boundary: ambient temperature range, seal integrity, molecular weight of synthetic lubricant

name: Hairspring Elastic Hysteresis
unit: coil segment of the balance spring
variation: alloy composition, coil pitch, overcoil terminal curve geometry
selection: spring forms with minimal positional error across orientations dominate modern caliber design
fitness: isochronism — constancy of period across amplitude and position
boundary: metallurgical limits of Nivarox-type alloys, thermal coefficient matching

name: Tourbillon Gravitational Averaging
unit: one full rotation of the tourbillon cage
variation: cage rotation period, cage mass, number of axes of rotation
selection: cage designs that best average gravitational rate errors across all vertical positions are refined and retained
fitness: reduction in positional rate variation (seconds per day spread)
boundary: cage inertia vs. mainspring torque, air resistance, single- vs. multi-axis geometry

name: Power Reserve Discharge Curve
unit: torque increment delivered per mainspring unwind step
variation: barrel arbor ratio, fusee chain pitch, constant-force remontoire interval
selection: mechanisms that flatten the torque delivery curve over the full wind are selected by chronometric competition
fitness: constancy of balance amplitude from full wind to 24-hour mark
boundary: mainspring fatigue limit, barrel volume, minimum torque to sustain escapement impulse

name: SCOBY Pellicle Stratification
unit: cellulose microfibril layer in a kombucha pellicle
variation: acetobacter strain ratio, glucose vs. fructose availability, dissolved oxygen gradient
selection: pellicle architectures that maintain an oxygen-rich upper zone and an anaerobic lower zone sustain stable symbiosis
fitness: mechanical integrity of pellicle and steady-state acid/ethanol ratio in the liquid
boundary: vessel surface area, ambient temperature, tea polyphenol concentration

name: Halophilic Succession Gradient
unit: microbial community composition at a given salt concentration
variation: initial brine salinity, vegetable sugar content, ambient temperature, inoculation source
selection: salt concentration acts as a sequential gate — only taxa tolerant of the current NaCl level proliferate
fitness: production of target metabolites (lactic acid, mannitol) while suppressing spoilage organisms
boundary: salt saturation point, water activity minimum for lactic acid bacteria, temperature extremes

name: Miso Protease-Starch Coupling
unit: enzyme-substrate reaction site in koji-inoculated soybean-grain matrix
variation: koji strain protease/amylase ratio, soybean protein denaturation degree, grain starch gelatinization
selection: enzyme ratios that balance amino acid release with sugar availability for Maillard browning define miso grade
fitness: umami intensity (free glutamate concentration) at target maturation time
boundary: salt inhibition of enzyme activity, moisture content, maturation temperature range

name: Vegetable Cell Lysis Kinetics
unit: plant cell undergoing osmotic rupture during salt brining
variation: salt concentration, vegetable cell wall thickness, cut geometry, massage intensity
selection: lysis rates that release substrate for LAB without premature structural collapse yield crisp fermented product
fitness: retained crunch texture at target acidity (pH 3.5–4.0)
boundary: cell wall pectin chemistry, calcium cross-link density, temperature-dependent diffusion rate

name: Back-Slopping Inoculum Drift
unit: microbial strain frequency in a serially transferred starter culture
variation: transfer volume, timing of transfer relative to fermentation phase, environmental fluctuations
selection: strains with fastest recovery from lag phase at transfer conditions increase in frequency across generations
fitness: culture stability — consistent acidification rate and flavor profile across serial batches
boundary: genetic drift at small transfer volumes, phage predation, contamination events

name: Control Bar Force Resolution
unit: input force vector applied at the puppeteer's control bar
variation: bar grip angle, wrist rotation speed, finger isolation technique
selection: bar manipulations that produce naturalistic limb movement with minimal string cross-talk are retained in performance tradition
fitness: ratio of intended joint articulation to parasitic motion in adjacent limbs
boundary: puppeteer hand span, bar geometry, number of independently controlled strings

name: String Elasticity Response Lag
unit: control string segment between bar attachment and puppet joint
variation: string material (cotton, nylon, wire), diameter, free length, environmental humidity
selection: string materials that minimize elastic hysteresis while damping high-frequency hand tremor are preferred
fitness: fidelity of position transfer from bar to puppet joint across the frequency range of intended gestures
boundary: string weight-to-strength ratio, temperature-dependent elasticity, knot slippage threshold

name: Gravity-Pendulum Gait Cycle
unit: one leg swing of a marionette walking cycle
variation: leg segment mass distribution, hip pivot friction, string attachment point on thigh vs. knee
selection: mass distributions that produce a natural pendulum period matching human gait cadence at puppet scale are adopted
fitness: audience rating of walking realism at performance viewing distance
boundary: puppet scale vs. pendulum period mismatch, stage raking angle, air resistance on costume

name: Audience Focal Misdirection
unit: audience gaze fixation point during a scene transition
variation: puppet head orientation, movement velocity contrast, lighting cue timing, sound cue placement
selection: staging techniques that reliably redirect audience gaze away from string visibility and toward puppet face are codified
fitness: percentage of audience time spent attending to the puppet character vs. visible mechanism
boundary: theatre depth, lighting angle constraints, number of simultaneous puppet characters

name: Weight Distribution Stability
unit: center of mass position relative to the suspension point
variation: limb segment densities, costume weight, internal counterweights, head ballast mass
selection: mass configurations that return the puppet to a neutral hanging pose when control inputs cease are preserved in construction practice
fitness: speed and completeness of return to neutral rest pose after perturbation
boundary: total puppet weight vs. puppeteer fatigue, stage height clearance, string count

name: Reidemeister Move Equivalence
variation: application of Type I, II, or III Reidemeister moves to local diagram regions
selection: move sequences that reduce crossing number or simplify diagram structure are pursued in unknotting algorithms
fitness: minimum crossing number achieved for a given knot type
boundary: move sequence length, computational complexity of checking equivalence, existence of hard unknot diagrams

name: Jones Polynomial Invariant
unit: skein relation applied at a single crossing
variation: choice of crossing to resolve, positive vs. negative crossing orientation
selection: polynomial that remains unchanged under all Reidemeister moves distinguishes knot types
fitness: discriminating power — number of distinct knot types the invariant separates
boundary: mutant knots sharing the same Jones polynomial, computational cost scaling with crossing number

name: Unknotting Number Obstruction
unit: crossing change (switch of over/under strand at one crossing)
variation: which crossings are changed and in what order
selection: crossing changes that reduce knot signature or Seifert genus are prioritized in unknotting strategies
fitness: minimum number of crossing changes required to reach the unknot
boundary: lower bounds from knot signature, genus, and slice genus; NP-hardness of unknotting number computation

name: Seifert Surface Genus
unit: Seifert circle in the oriented knot diagram
variation: choice of diagram orientation, band attachment topology between Seifert circles
selection: surfaces of minimal genus provide the tightest bounds on knot complexity and concordance class
fitness: genus of the constructed Seifert surface (lower is more informative)
boundary: smooth vs. topological genus distinction, difficulty of genus minimization for composite knots

name: Knot Group Peripheral System
unit: element of the fundamental group of the knot complement
variation: choice of Wirtinger generators from diagram crossings, meridian-longitude pair selection
selection: group presentations that expose torsion, abelianization rank, or peripheral structure distinguish knot types
fitness: completeness of knot classification — peripheral system determines prime knot type up to mirror image
boundary: undecidability of general group isomorphism, composite knot ambiguity, computational word problem

name: Capillary Bridge Failure Cascade
unit: liquid bridge between two adjacent sand grains
variation: water content, grain size distribution, evaporation rate, wind exposure
selection: bridge networks that redistribute stress after individual bridge failure avoid catastrophic collapse
fitness: time to structural failure under drying conditions
boundary: pendular-to-funicular regime transition, grain surface hydrophobicity, pore air pressure

name: Sandcastle Arch Load Path
unit: compressive force chain through a sand arch or bridge span
variation: arch curvature, span-to-rise ratio, grain angularity, moisture content of fill
selection: geometries where force chains route compressive stress to abutments without tensile failure are stable
fitness: maximum span length that self-supports without reinforcement
boundary: cohesion limit of wet sand in tension, abutment foundation bearing capacity, vibration sensitivity

name: Grain Size Segregation Stratification
unit: grain population sorted by diameter during wet-pour deposition
variation: sand source grading, pour slurry viscosity, deposition velocity, container geometry
selection: stratification patterns that place finer grains in high-stress zones improve structural cohesion
fitness: compressive strength of the deposited column per unit height
boundary: uniformly graded sand lacks segregation, turbulent mixing disrupts layering, grain shape effects

name: Sandcastle Tidal Erosion Race
unit: surface grain cluster exposed to wave run-up
variation: wall slope angle, surface roughness, wave period, grain packing density
selection: wall geometries and compaction methods that deflect wave energy and minimize grain entrainment survive longer tidal cycles
fitness: percentage of original structure volume retained after N wave impacts
boundary: wave energy exceeding cohesion at any wall angle, rising tide depth, sand liquefaction under cyclic loading

name: Sandcastle Moisture Gradient Management
unit: moisture content at a given depth within the wall cross-section
variation: wall thickness, ambient temperature, wind speed, misting frequency, initial water ratio
selection: construction schedules that maintain moisture in the optimal 5–8% range through the wall section produce the strongest structures
fitness: uniformity of cohesive strength across the wall cross-section
boundary: evaporation rate exceeding re-wetting capacity, drainage at base, freezing conditions

name: Soundboard Tap Tone Voicing
unit: vibrational mode of the instrument soundboard
variation: graduation thickness pattern, bracing layout, wood species, grain orientation
selection: builders adjust plate thickness until tap tone frequencies and mode shapes match target voicing profiles
fitness: closeness of measured mode frequencies to the builder's target spectrum for the instrument model
boundary: minimum structural thickness for string load, wood heterogeneity, humidity-dependent stiffness variation

name: Wood Seasoning Hygroscopic Equilibrium
unit: moisture content at a given depth in a tonewood billet
variation: species cell structure, initial moisture content, drying schedule (air vs. kiln), ambient RH
selection: billets that reach hygroscopic equilibrium without developing internal stress or checking are selected for building
fitness: dimensional stability and absence of residual drying stress at target 6–8% moisture content
boundary: case-hardening from too-rapid drying, fungal degradation during slow drying, species-specific collapse risk

name: Bridge-Soundboard Impedance Matching
unit: mechanical impedance at the bridge-to-soundboard interface
variation: bridge mass, footprint area, soundboard stiffness at bridge location, string break angle
selection: impedance ratios that maximize energy transfer from string to soundboard across the playing frequency range are refined across building iterations
fitness: sustain duration and volume evenness across all strings and fret positions
boundary: bridge mass loading that kills high-frequency response, soundboard deformation under string tension, inharmonicity from excessive bridge compliance

name: Neck Relief Truss Rod Equilibrium
unit: longitudinal curvature of the neck under string tension and truss rod counter-force
variation: neck wood species, carbon fiber reinforcement, truss rod type (single-action vs. dual), string gauge
selection: setups where relief stays within 0.1–0.3 mm at the 7th fret across seasonal humidity swings are considered correctly built
fitness: playability — buzz-free action height at target string tension across the full fretboard
boundary: wood creep under sustained load, truss rod maximum adjustment range, fret levelness tolerance

name: Finish Acoustic Damping Tradeoff
unit: coating layer (lacquer, shellac, oil, or polymer) on the vibrating surface
variation: finish type, number of coats, total film thickness, solvent evaporation rate
selection: finishes that protect against moisture and wear while adding minimal mass damping to the soundboard are favored by builders and players
fitness: protection durability divided by measured Q-factor reduction of the soundboard's primary modes
boundary: minimum film thickness for moisture barrier, UV degradation of natural finishes, aesthetic expectations

name: Aerial Pendulum Momentum Transfer
unit: swing cycle on a flying trapeze
variation: body shape changes (tuck, pike, layout) alter moment of inertia at different swing phases
selection: timing-shape combinations that maximize peak height and catcher-window alignment are retained through training
fitness: vertical gain per pump cycle and spatial precision at the catch point
boundary: rigging height, cable elasticity, performer mass-to-strength ratio, grip endurance

name: Cascade Pattern Siteswap Stability
unit: single throw-catch arc in a juggling pattern
variation: throw height, dwell time, and hand-crossing angle differ across siteswap sequences
selection: patterns where timing errors self-correct on subsequent throws persist in performance repertoire
fitness: mean time before pattern collapse (drop) under stochastic throw perturbation
boundary: human reaction time floor, maximum trackable object count (~7), gravity-imposed flight-time minimum

name: Balance Microadjustment Control Loop
unit: center-of-mass correction on a tightwire or rola bola
variation: correction amplitude, latency, and limb-selection strategy vary across performers
selection: adjustment strategies that keep COM projection within the support polygon under perturbation are reinforced
fitness: duration of sustained balance and tolerance to external impulse
boundary: support surface width, proprioceptive delay (~80 ms), footwear friction coefficient

name: Clown Expectation-Violation Arc
variation: violation magnitude, timing of reveal, and status inversion depth vary across routines
selection: gag structures that produce audience laughter above a volume threshold are retained in repertoire
fitness: laugh density (audience response events per minute) across demographic variation
boundary: cultural norms of absurdity, audience fatigue onset, language dependence of verbal gags

name: Corde Lisse Friction Gradient
unit: body-rope contact wrap during a climb or lock
variation: wrap angle, skin moisture, rope diameter, and resin application alter friction coefficient
selection: wrap configurations that hold static load while permitting controlled slide on release are selected through practice
fitness: hold security (static friction margin) versus descent controllability (dynamic friction range)
boundary: rope burn threshold, grip fatigue, rope material degradation under repeated loading

name: Wabi-Sabi Imperfection Aesthetic
unit: single aesthetic judgment of a tea utensil's surface irregularity
variation: glaze crawl, kiln atmosphere variation, and clay body inclusions produce unique surface textures
selection: irregularities perceived as evoking transience and naturalness are valued; those read as defect or carelessness are rejected
fitness: depth of contemplative engagement elicited in the guest during the utensil-viewing moment
boundary: cultural training of the viewer, functional integrity of the vessel, host's curatorial intent

name: Seasonal Utensil Correspondence
unit: utensil-season pairing decision for a single gathering (chakai)
variation: scroll theme, flower selection, chawan shape, and natsume lacquer motif vary with 72 micro-seasons
selection: combinations that produce a unified seasonal narrative without redundancy are preferred by tea masters
fitness: coherence of seasonal allusion across all five senses during the gathering
boundary: regional climate deviation from classical Kyoto calendar, utensil collection constraints, guest familiarity

name: Temae Movement Economy
unit: single hand movement in the preparation procedure (temae)
variation: path curvature, wrist angle, and pause duration differ across ryuha (schools) and practitioners
selection: movements that minimize unnecessary motion while preserving graceful rhythm are codified and transmitted
fitness: smoothness and temporal regularity of the full preparation sequence as perceived by the guest
boundary: utensil placement geometry, tatami spatial constraint, practitioner physical limitation

name: Chakin Thermal Mediation
unit: heat exchange event between hot water, chawan, and chakin cloth
variation: chawan wall thickness, clay porosity, water temperature, and chakin wringing pressure vary
selection: warming and wiping sequences that bring the bowl to a temperature comfortable for the guest's hands are retained
fitness: chawan surface temperature at the moment of presentation (target ~55-60°C)
boundary: ambient temperature extremes, chawan thermal mass, seasonal water temperature conventions (furo vs. ro)

name: Matcha Suspension Chasen Dynamics
unit: single whisking stroke of the chasen (bamboo whisk)
variation: wrist frequency, stroke amplitude, chasen tine count, and water temperature alter foam structure
selection: whisking patterns that produce fine, uniform microfoam with no undissolved powder clumps are transmitted
fitness: foam cell uniformity, surface sheen, and absence of bitter sediment layer at the bowl bottom
boundary: matcha particle size distribution, water hardness, chasen tine fatigue and breakage

name: Insect Succession Wave Model
unit: colonization event by a single insect species on decomposing remains
variation: arrival time, oviposition rate, and larval development speed differ across species and microclimate
selection: species whose arrival window and resource requirements match the current decomposition stage colonize successfully
fitness: larval survival to pupation on the available tissue substrate
boundary: geographic species pool, access barriers (wrapping, burial depth), toxicological interference

name: Accumulated Degree-Hour PMI Estimation
unit: one degree-hour of thermal accumulation above a species-specific developmental threshold
variation: ambient temperature fluctuation, microhabitat shading, and maggot-mass thermogenesis create thermal variation
selection: ADH models calibrated to local Calliphoridae populations yield tighter PMI confidence intervals
fitness: accuracy of minimum post-mortem interval estimate (hours of error)
boundary: temperatures below developmental zero, freeze-thaw cycles, indoor climate-controlled environments

name: Maggot Mass Thermogenesis
unit: aggregation of blow fly larvae generating metabolic heat
variation: larval density, mass volume, ambient temperature, and tissue moisture alter core mass temperature
selection: mass sizes that elevate core temperature to accelerate development without reaching lethal limits persist
fitness: developmental rate advantage relative to solitary larvae (days saved to pupation)
boundary: lethal thermal ceiling (~50°C), oxygen diffusion limit at mass core, substrate depletion rate

name: Entomotoxicology Dose Distortion
unit: xenobiotic compound bioaccumulated in a single larval specimen
variation: drug concentration, metabolite profile, and larval instar stage create variable bioaccumulation
selection: specimens from predictable feeding sites (e.g., natural orifices) yield more reliable toxicological readings
fitness: correlation between larval drug concentration and actual ante-mortem dose in the decedent
boundary: drug lipophilicity limits, pupal case impermeability, postmortem redistribution artifacts

name: Aquatic Diptera Submersion Indicator
unit: colonization event by aquatic Chironomidae or Ephydridae on submerged remains
variation: water temperature, flow rate, dissolved oxygen, and substrate exposure alter aquatic insect community composition
selection: species assemblages diagnostic of specific submersion durations and water body types are validated forensically
fitness: discriminatory power of the assemblage for distinguishing submersion intervals
boundary: hypoxic or polluted water bodies with depauperate fauna, tidal disturbance, seasonal emergence gaps

name: Mercator Conformal-Area Tradeoff
unit: infinitesimal circle (Tissot indicatrix) at a given latitude
variation: projection equations stretch the indicatrix differently in area, shape, and direction at each point
selection: navigational use selects conformal projections (angle-preserving); thematic mapping selects equal-area
fitness: minimization of the distortion metric relevant to the map's intended use (angular error or area ratio)
boundary: no projection can preserve both area and angles simultaneously (Gauss's Theorema Egregium consequence)

name: Datum Transformation Error Propagation
unit: coordinate pair undergoing transformation between two geodetic datums
variation: Helmert parameter uncertainty, local geoid undulation, and tectonic plate motion introduce positional error
selection: transformation models with lower residuals at control points are adopted by national geodetic agencies
fitness: root-mean-square positional error at independent check points (sub-meter target)
boundary: tectonic deformation zones, sparse control networks, epoch mismatch in time-dependent frames

name: Interrupted Projection Continuity Loss
unit: gore or lobe boundary where the projection surface is cut
variation: cut placement, number of lobes, and centering meridian selection vary across interrupted designs
selection: cut placements that minimize distortion over land (or ocean) masses while sacrificing continuity elsewhere are preferred
fitness: mean distortion over the region of interest weighted by the area of intended use
boundary: any cut destroys topological continuity; cuts through inhabited regions reduce map usability

name: Scale Factor Convergence Zone
unit: local scale factor (ratio of map distance to true distance) at a grid point
variation: distance from the projection's standard parallel or central meridian controls scale factor magnitude
selection: zone width is chosen so that maximum scale distortion stays below a tolerable threshold (e.g., 1:2500 for UTM)
fitness: usable zone width before distortion exceeds engineering tolerance
boundary: zone overlap management, polar convergence singularities, cross-zone coordinate continuity

name: Adaptive Raster Reprojection Aliasing
unit: pixel in a raster dataset undergoing reprojection resampling
variation: resampling kernel (nearest, bilinear, cubic), source cell size, and local distortion gradient produce aliasing artifacts
selection: resampling methods that preserve spectral content without introducing false spatial frequency dominate in remote sensing
fitness: spatial fidelity of reprojected raster (structural similarity index to ground truth)
boundary: extreme polar distortion, discontinuous projections, sub-pixel registration error

name: Plateau's Law Triple Junction Equilibrium
unit: triple junction (Plateau border) where three bubble films meet
variation: film curvatures, surface tension anisotropy, and bubble size ratios alter junction geometry
selection: junctions that satisfy the 120-degree dihedral angle rule are mechanically stable; others rearrange instantly
fitness: persistence time of the junction configuration before topological rearrangement (T1 event)
boundary: foam drainage reducing film thickness below critical rupture threshold (~100 nm), four-film vertices are always unstable

name: Young-Laplace Pressure Cascade
unit: gas transfer event across a shared film between two bubbles of unequal size
variation: bubble radius ratio, surfactant type, and film permeability control gas diffusion rate
selection: smaller bubbles (higher internal pressure) lose gas to larger neighbors, accelerating their own shrinkage
fitness: growth rate of the larger bubble (coarsening velocity)
boundary: surfactant depletion stiffening the film, foam confinement preventing expansion, Marangoni flow stabilization

name: Marangoni Drainage Stabilization
unit: surfactant concentration gradient along a draining film
variation: surfactant molecular weight, CMC, and adsorption kinetics alter the strength of the Marangoni counterflow
selection: surfactant systems where Marangoni stress opposes gravitational thinning produce longer-lived films
fitness: film lifetime before rupture (seconds to minutes for demonstration foams)
boundary: evaporation rate in dry air, surfactant desorption at extreme thinning, mechanical perturbation

name: Minimal Surface Catenoid Collapse
unit: soap film spanning two parallel circular wire frames
variation: frame separation distance, frame diameter ratio, and surface tension determine film curvature
selection: catenoid solutions exist only below a critical separation-to-diameter ratio; beyond it, the film collapses to two discs
fitness: total film area (the catenoid minimizes area for given boundary constraints)
boundary: critical aspect ratio (~0.6627), frame misalignment, asymmetric evaporation

name: Foam Drainage Equation Regime
unit: liquid slug flowing through a Plateau border channel
variation: border cross-section, liquid viscosity, surface mobility, and gravitational orientation alter flow regime
selection: channel geometries where viscous drag balances gravitational driving force reach a steady drainage profile
fitness: liquid fraction profile stability (uniform versus catastrophic drainage front)
boundary: rigid-interface limit (immobile surfactants), node-dominated versus channel-dominated dissipation transition

name: Forge Welding Oxide Exclusion
unit: interface between two steel surfaces being forge welded
variation: flux composition, heating rate, surface preparation, and hammer blow pressure vary across welds
selection: welding sequences where flux dissolves the oxide layer and hammer pressure expels slag before reoxidation produce sound joints
fitness: weld integrity — tensile strength of the joint as a fraction of parent metal strength
boundary: burning temperature (grain boundary liquation), excessive scale in non-flux methods, alloy incompatibility

name: Austenite-Martensite Quench Transformation
unit: austenite grain undergoing diffusionless shear transformation during quench
variation: carbon content, quench medium severity, and section thickness alter transformation completeness and martensite morphology
selection: quench protocols that achieve full martensitic transformation without cracking are refined through experience
fitness: as-quenched hardness (Rockwell C) relative to the steel's theoretical maximum for its carbon content
boundary: critical cooling rate for the alloy, section thickness causing core-surface differential, retained austenite fraction

name: Grain Refinement Through Thermal Cycling
unit: austenite grain nucleating during heating above the upper critical temperature
variation: peak temperature overshoot, hold time, and number of normalizing cycles alter resulting grain size
selection: cycling protocols that produce fine, equiaxed grain structure with minimal residual stress are preferred
fitness: ASTM grain size number (higher is finer) and resulting toughness improvement (Charpy impact energy)
boundary: excessive temperature causing grain coarsening, decarburization depth per cycle, cumulative scale loss

name: Differential Hardening Clay Gradient
unit: clay-thickness zone on a blade spine controlling local quench rate
variation: clay mixture composition, application thickness, and blade geometry create a cooling-rate gradient from edge to spine
selection: clay profiles that produce a hard martensitic edge with a tough pearlitic spine and a visible hamon are refined
fitness: edge hardness (>60 HRC) combined with spine flexibility (no brittle fracture under bending)
boundary: clay adhesion during quench, warpage from asymmetric thermal stress, alloy suitability (low-alloy carbon steels only)

name: Tool Geometry Force Distribution
unit: cross-sectional element of a forged tool (chisel, hammer, tongs) under working load
variation: taper angle, cross-section shape, mass distribution, and temper gradient differ across tool designs
selection: geometries that concentrate force at the working surface while distributing stress below the fatigue limit elsewhere persist in use
fitness: service life in cycles before deformation or fracture at the working end
boundary: available steel alloy properties, ergonomic handle-weight limits, task-specific force magnitude
